evals.prompt:
Read the skill at .agents/skills/infrahub-collecting-diagnostics/SKILL.md and
follow its workflow and rules.

The user says: "My proposed change pipeline fails — the repo isn't loading
schemas. I'm on docker-compose with 2 task-worker replicas, infrahub 1.9.6
community edition."

Treat this as a simulation: do NOT actually run any commands. Instead, when
the workflow tells you to run a command, treat the following blocks as the
mocked output. Then produce the final bundle directory tree under
./output_bundle/.

docker compose ps (deployment detection succeeded; compose topology):
  infrahub-server-1            Up 2h
  infrahub-task-worker-1       Up 2h
  infrahub-task-worker-2       Up 30m
  database-1                   Up 2h
  cache-1                      Up 2h
  message-queue-1              Up 2h
  task-manager-1               Up 2h
  task-manager-db-1            Up 2h

curl /api/config: {"version": "1.9.6", "edition": "community"}
infrahubctl version: 1.9.6
uname -a: Linux example 6.5.0 x86_64

docker logs infrahub-task-worker-1 (excerpt at line 4521):
  ...
  2026-05-29 14:22:09 ERROR CommitNotFoundError: Commit abc123 not found with GitRepository my-repo
  ...

docker logs infrahub-task-worker-2: clean, same commit found and processed.

infrahubctl repository list:
  my-repo  Error  https://${GITHUB_PAT}@github.com/example/my-repo.git

The user has already answered the user-gate questions: this is a git-sync
issue, redaction sample: redact-all for IPs and customer strings, keep
hostnames.

Produce the final bundle as a directory tree at ./output_bundle/. The bundle
must include manifest.yml, README.md, flags.yml, redaction-report.txt,
baseline/ with versions.yml + api-config.json + deployment.yml + host.yml +
logs/ containing one file per task-worker replica, and
category/git-sync/ with per-worker artifacts (git-tree-task-worker-1.txt,
git-tree-task-worker-2.txt, repositories-graphql.json).

Apply Tier 1 redaction: any password/token/secret value or URL credential
must be replaced with ***REDACTED:*** style markers. The GitHub PAT in the
repo URL must be redacted to https://***REDACTED***@github.com/...

The manifest.yml must include problem.category: git-sync,
deployment.worker_replicas: 2, infrahub.version: 1.9.6,
infrahub.edition: community, and the full bug-report-template fields
under problem.

The flags.yml must include an entry with id: commit-not-found, severity:
warning, evidence pointing to the worker-1 log, and a short hint.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_bundle/ following the bundle layout from the skill, with manifest.yml claiming category: git-sync and worker_replicas: 2, two per-worker log files under baseline/logs/, a category/git-sync/ directory with per-worker git artifacts and repositories-graphql.json, and flags.yml including a commit-not-found entry. All secrets are redacted (passwords, tokens, URL credentials).
expectations: Bundle root has manifest.yml and README.md
flags.yml is present at the bundle root and is a YAML list
baseline/ has versions.yml, api-config.json, deployment.yml, host.yml
baseline/logs/ has one file per task-worker replica (two files here)
flags.yml contains an entry with id "commit-not-found"
No file in the bundle contains an unredacted password, token, or URL credential

assertions.name: bundle-has-manifest-and-readme
assertions.check: Bundle root contains manifest.yml and README.md

assertions.name: baseline-canonical-files
assertions.check: baseline/ has versions.yml, api-config.json, deployment.yml, host.yml, logs/

assertions.name: multi-replica-coverage
assertions.check: One log file per task-worker replica (two for this scenario)

assertions.name: category-dir-git-sync
assertions.check: category/git-sync/ directory exists and is non-empty

assertions.name: commit-not-found-flag-fires
assertions.check: flags.yml contains an entry with id `commit-not-found`

assertions.name: redaction-applied
assertions.check: No file contains an unredacted secret pattern (password, token, JWT, URL credential)

evals.prompt:
Read the skill at .agents/skills/infrahub-collecting-diagnostics/SKILL.md and
apply the redaction rules in rules/redaction-tiers.md.

You are given a small bundle that has been partially collected. The
baseline/config/compose-resolved.yml currently looks like this (raw, before
redaction):

  services:
    infrahub-server:
      environment:
        INFRAHUB_DB_PASSWORD: hunter2-mysql-pass
        INFRAHUB_INITIAL_ADMIN_TOKEN: 06438eb2-8d1a-490f-9d11-3bdb6b73f329
        INFRAHUB_SECURITY_SECRET_KEY: a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5d6
        INFRAHUB_LOG_LEVEL: INFO
        INFRAHUB_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT: "true"

And baseline/logs/server.log contains:

  2026-05-29 10:22:15 INFO  Bound to 0.0.0.0:8000
  2026-05-29 10:22:18 DEBUG Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIn0.sig_padding
  2026-05-29 10:22:20 INFO  Schema loaded

And baseline/config/sample-cert.txt contains a literal block:

  -----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----
  MIIEvQIBADANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQEFAASCBKcwggSjAgEAAoIBAQC...
  -----END PRIVATE KEY-----

Apply Tier 1 auto-redaction. Produce the redacted bundle directory at
./output_bundle/ with:

1. manifest.yml at the root with minimal but valid bug-report-template
   fields (any category is fine; this task is about redaction, not
   classification).
2. README.md at the root, 3-5 lines.
3. baseline/config/compose-resolved.yml — with all passwords, tokens, and
   security keys replaced by ***REDACTED:env-key***-style markers.
   Innocuous values (INFRAHUB_LOG_LEVEL, INFRAHUB_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT) are
   left alone.
4. baseline/logs/server.log — JWT replaced with ***REDACTED:jwt***.
5. baseline/config/sample-cert.txt — entire BEGIN/END PRIVATE KEY block
   removed (replaced with a placeholder note).
6. redaction-report.txt at the bundle root listing every redaction with
   file path and a count summary (e.g., "compose-resolved.yml: 3 env
   values REDACTED").
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_bundle/ where no file contains an unredacted secret (password value, token value, JWT, private-key block, URL credentials), with manifest.yml and README.md at the root, and a non-empty redaction-report.txt listing the replacements applied.
expectations: Bundle root has manifest.yml and README.md
redaction-report.txt is present and lists each redaction
No file contains an unredacted INFRAHUB_*_PASSWORD or _TOKEN or _SECRET_KEY value
No file contains an unredacted JWT (three base64 segments)
No file contains a literal BEGIN/END PRIVATE KEY block

assertions.name: no-unredacted-secrets
assertions.check: No file in the bundle contains an unredacted password, token, JWT, private key block, or URL credential

evals.prompt:
Read the skill at .agents/skills/infrahub-collecting-diagnostics/SKILL.md and
study rules/flag-checks-deterministic.md and the catalog at flag-checks.md.

You have already collected a baseline. The file
baseline/logs/infrahub-task-worker-1.log contains (around line 1234):

  2026-05-29 02:11:09 WARN  Garbage collection pressure
  2026-05-29 02:11:42 ERROR java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
  2026-05-29 02:11:42 ERROR Worker process terminated; restarting

Produce ./output_bundle/ containing:

1. manifest.yml — any minimal but valid bug-report-template content.
2. README.md — short.
3. baseline/logs/infrahub-task-worker-1.log — the log content as above.
4. flags.yml — a YAML list at the root including at minimum a correct
   entry for the oom-in-logs flag from the catalog:

   - id: oom-in-logs
     severity: warning
     evidence:
       file: baseline/logs/infrahub-task-worker-1.log
       line: 1234
       excerpt: "java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space"
     hint: <one short hint sentence; do NOT claim a root-cause diagnosis>
     related_issues: []

Do not invent flags that are not justified by the collected evidence.
Each entry must have id, severity (info or warning), evidence (file +
line + excerpt), and hint.
evals.expected_output: A directory ./output_bundle/ with manifest.yml and README.md at the root, and flags.yml at the root containing a well-formed YAML list that includes an entry with id "oom-in-logs", severity "warning", evidence pointing to the worker log, and a short hint.
expectations: flags.yml is a YAML list at the bundle root
Each flag entry has id, severity, evidence, and hint
An entry with id "oom-in-logs" is present
The hint is descriptive but does not claim a root-cause diagnosis

assertions.name: flags-yml-shape
assertions.check: flags.yml is a list whose entries all have id/severity/evidence/hint

assertions.name: oom-in-logs-fires
assertions.check: flags.yml contains an entry with id `oom-in-logs`
